title: Avoid Fancy Naming
excerpt: Simple names reduce onboarding time. If naming sounds clever, it is probably unclear.

title: The Architecture of Boredom
excerpt: Great systems often feel boring to maintain. Boring is a feature when uptime matters.

title: Text First Design
excerpt: If the words are weak, no styling can save the page. Strong text can survive plain styling.

title: Ship Fewer Features
excerpt: Complexity grows faster than value. Each feature should defend its existence.

title: Learning by Rebuilding
excerpt: Rebuilding tiny tools teaches architecture faster than passive tutorials. Make it small enough to finish.

title: Sharpen the Question
excerpt: Before solving, sharpen the question. A precise question removes half the work.

title: The Quiet Advantage
excerpt: Calm execution is underrated. Most outcomes come from steady work, not loud announcements.

title: The Cost of Context Switching
excerpt: Switching tasks feels productive because it feels busy. But it kills deep work.

title: Against Dashboard Culture
excerpt: Not everything needs a chart. Sometimes a short paragraph with one decision is enough.

title: Simple Sites Age Well
excerpt: Static pages with clear text outlive trendy redesigns. I want this site to still feel readable years later.
